prompt:
﻿Use the image just shown as the visual reference for art style only: match its 16-bit arcade pixel art, crisp chunky pixels, 1px dark outline color #141019, flat 2-3 step shading, warm lantern-gold highlights, and clean readable silhouettes. Do not copy the miner character.

Create exactly one square 1x1 sprite sheet tile on a 100% solid flat #FF00FF magenta background. One single mining platformer prop only: a polished timber mining chute slide running diagonally at exactly 45 degrees from the top-left corner region down to the bottom-right corner region of the square tile. Side-view platformer readability, but the tile itself is square and collision-aligned.

The chute must be seamless when copies are placed diagonally with each next copy offset one full tile right and one full tile down: the chute enters the tile at the top-left corner region and exits at the bottom-right corner region with the same width, same rail spacing, same surface centerline, same outline thickness, and same cross-section alignment. The diagonal slide should be cropped cleanly by the square tile boundary at those two matching corner regions so the next tile continues perfectly. Keep the centerline on a true 45-degree slope from top-left to bottom-right.

Visual details: low timber side rails, smooth worn sliding surface, timber brown base #6e4a2d, lantern-gold worn sheen #ffd24a down the middle, dark cavern-brown shadows, subtle bashful pink accent pixels only if needed, no metal track, no nails dominating. 1px #141019 dark outline around the chute and rails. Flat pixel shading, no painterly gradients, no anti-aliased blur.

Containment and cleanup rules: the only non-magenta pixels are the chute itself; no other props, no dust, no floating effects. Leave the rest solid #FF00FF. No text, no labels, no UI, no borders, no frame lines, no shadows on the background.

postprocess_overrides.reason: diagonal repeat tile must keep generated edge contact instead of crop-centering the prop
